terms.plain: A bright spot beside the sun, also called a sundog. Its offset can recover sun altitude when the anchors are eligible.
The common ring around the sun. Sundog uses it as the scale reference for the atlas.
A larger halo used as context for several high-arc families. It is rendered as vocabulary, not as the promoted inverse handle.
The high smile-shaped arc above the sun. Its visibility is gated by observer geometry.
A high-sun plate-crystal arc sometimes called a fire rainbow. It is named in the legend but outside the current sundog-facing regime.
An arc from column-crystal geometry near the 22 degree halo. It is useful vocabulary, not a promoted proof route.
The horizontal belt through the sun and parhelia. It is rendered in the atlas but not promoted as a hidden-state handle.
